task_completion.description: Log in to Moodle using admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Moodle dashboard or homepage showing the user is logged in as 'admin' (e.g., top right corner profile).
The Moodle interface displays the 'World History' course page, with 'HIST201' or 'World History' visible in the header or breadcrumbs.

task_completion.description: Access the permissions or role override settings specifically for the course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Permissions' or 'Users' administration page is open within the context of the 'World History' course.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Student' role to override its permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Override permissions in Course: World History' (or similar) is visible, specifically showing that the 'Student' role is being edited.
The capability 'View participants' (moodle/course:viewparticipants) is visible with the 'Prevent' radio button selected, or a confirmation message indicates the permission was successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Moodle web GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating Moodle, not a terminal using curl, python scripts, or database commands to change permissions.

integrity.description: The permission change was applied at the course context, not globally.
integrity.visual_evidence: The breadcrumbs or page title must indicate 'Course: World History' rather than 'Site administration > Users > Permissions > Define roles'.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard Moodle interface and styling, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Course: 'World History' (shortname: HIST201). Target Role: 'Student'. Target Capability: 'View participants' (moodle/course:viewparticipants). The permission must be set to 'Prevent' (UI radio button). Crucially, this must be done in the Course context (Override permissions in Course: World History), NOT in the global Site Administration (Define roles) context.
